violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: multiple (check-sat) calls → multi-line Z3 output →
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 33
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: multiple (check-sat) calls → multi-line Z3 output →
; classified as "error" even when the formula is satisfiable.
; extractSmt2Blocks only requires ≥1 (check-sat); verifyBlock receives smt2 verbatim.
; Code transition chain: num_check_sat → output_line_count → output_type → result_type.

(declare-const num_check_sat Int)      ; # of (check-sat) calls in the smt2 string
(declare-const output_line_count Int)  ; Z3 outputs exactly one result line per (check-sat)
(declare-const output_type Int)        ; 0 = exactly "sat"; 3 = multiline/other
(declare-const result_type Int)        ; 0 = "sat"; 3 = "error"
(declare-const z3_first_verdict Int)   ; Z3's actual verdict for the first check-sat: 0 = sat

; Code model: Z3 emits one output line per (check-sat) invocation
(assert (= output_line_count num_check_sat))

; Calling context: extractSmt2Blocks passes blocks with ≥1 check-sat (allows multiples)
(assert (>= num_check_sat 1))

; Concrete witness: two (check-sat) calls in the formula
(assert (= num_check_sat 2))

; Code transition: output_line_count > 1 → internal newlines survive .trim()
; → output cannot equal "sat", "unsat", or "unknown" → output_type = 3
(assert (> output_line_count 1))
(assert (= output_type 3))

; Code transition (if-chain): output_type = 3 → all exact-match checks fail
; → fallthrough: return { result: "error", error: output } → result_type = 3
(assert (= result_type 3))

; Z3's actual verdict for the first check-sat was satisfiable
(assert (= z3_first_verdict 0))

; Contract violation: name-promise requires result = "sat" when formula is satisfiable,
; but result_type = 3 ("error") while z3_first_verdict = 0 ("sat")
(assert (not (= result_type z3_first_verdict)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: num_check_sat=2, output_line_count=2, output_type=3 (multiline),
; result_type=3 ("error"), z3_first_verdict=0 ("sat") → satisfiable formula
; misclassified as "error" because multi-line output fails all exact-match arms

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: catch block has no arm for Z3 "unknown" output.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 33
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: catch block has no arm for Z3 "unknown" output.
; When Z3's -T:5 soft timeout fires and Z3 exits non-zero, execSync throws.
; The catch block checks stdout == "sat" and stdout == "unsat" but NOT "unknown".
; "unknown" falls to the else branch: result = "error" instead of "unknown".
; This violates the contract: faithfully relay Z3's verdict.

(declare-const stdout_type Int)        ; 0 = "sat", 1 = "unsat", 2 = "unknown", 3 = other
(declare-const catch_result_type Int)  ; 0 = "sat", 1 = "unsat", 2 = "unknown", 3 = "error"
(declare-const z3_nonzero_exit Int)    ; 1 = Z3 exited non-zero → execSync threw

; Precondition: execSync threw because Z3 exited non-zero → catch block is active
(assert (= z3_nonzero_exit 1))

; Code transition (catch block, lines 46–50 of verifyBlock):
;   if stdout == "sat"   → result = "sat"
;   if stdout == "unsat" → result = "unsat"
;   else                 → result = "error"   ← MISSING arm for "unknown"
(assert (or
  (and (= stdout_type 0) (= catch_result_type 0))
  (and (= stdout_type 1) (= catch_result_type 1))
  (and (not (= stdout_type 0)) (not (= stdout_type 1)) (= catch_result_type 3))
))

; Z3's -T:5 timeout: Z3 outputs "unknown" to stdout before exiting non-zero
(assert (= stdout_type 2))

; Code transition consequence: stdout_type = 2 → not "sat", not "unsat"
; → else branch fires → catch_result_type = 3 ("error")
(assert (= catch_result_type 3))

; Contract violation: Z3 returned "unknown" (encoded as 2) but verifyBlock
; reports "error" (encoded as 3); 2 ≠ 3
(assert (not (= catch_result_type stdout_type)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: z3_nonzero_exit=1, stdout_type=2 ("unknown"), catch_result_type=3 ("error")
; Z3 soft-timeout output "unknown" is misclassified as "error" in the catch block

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: verifyAll does not establish verifyBlock's implicit
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 33
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: verifyAll does not establish verifyBlock's implicit
; single-(check-sat) precondition before calling it.
; verifyBlock returns correct results only when smt2 has exactly one (check-sat).
; verifyAll's two filters (extractSmt2Blocks, isVacuous) do not enforce this.
;
; Code transitions modelled:
;   extractSmt2Blocks: passes block iff check_sat_count >= 1
;   isVacuous filter:  not vacuous iff has_transitions = 1 (ignores check_sat_count)
;   verifyAll:         calls verifyBlock iff both filters pass

(declare-const check_sat_count Int)        ; # of (check-sat) in the smt2 string
(declare-const has_transitions Int)        ; 1 = block has multi-variable assertions
(declare-const passes_extract_filter Int)  ; 1 = passes extractSmt2Blocks filter
(declare-const passes_vacuous_filter Int)  ; 1 = passes isVacuous filter (not vacuous)
(declare-const reaches_verify_block Int)   ; 1 = verifyBlock is called with this smt2

; Code transition (extractSmt2Blocks, ~line 21): passes iff ≥1 (check-sat) present
(assert (=> (>= check_sat_count 1) (= passes_extract_filter 1)))

; Code transition (isVacuous, lines 63–88): not vacuous iff transition assertions exist
(assert (=> (= has_transitions 1) (= passes_vacuous_filter 1)))

; Code transition (verifyAll, ~line 113): calls verifyBlock iff both filters pass
(assert (=> (and (= passes_extract_filter 1) (= passes_vacuous_filter 1))
            (= reaches_verify_block 1)))

; Concrete witness: smt2 with 2 (check-sat) calls AND transition assertions
(assert (= check_sat_count 2))
(assert (= has_transitions 1))

; Violation: verifyBlock is reached (= 1) despite check_sat_count > 1;
; the single-check-sat precondition is not established by the caller
(assert (> check_sat_count 1))
(assert (= reaches_verify_block 1))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: check_sat_count=2, has_transitions=1 → passes_extract_filter=1,
; passes_vacuous_filter=1 → reaches_verify_block=1
; verifyBlock is called without its implicit single-(check-sat) precondition established
